title: Conjecture 1.3 (Conlon, Fox, Sudakov) / Sparse Linear Conjecture
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$ contains a pure $(\varepsilon|G|, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair.
context_text: Stated as problem 3.13 in [4], this asserts that every $H$-free graph contains two disjoint sets of linear size that are either completely joined or anticomplete. It is equivalent to Conjecture 1.4 (the sparse linear conjecture) via a theorem of Rödl. The sparse linear conjecture form states that either some vertex has degree at least $\varepsilon|G|$, or there are two disjoint anticomplete sets of sizes at least $\varepsilon|G|^\varepsilon$ and $\varepsilon|G|$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all almost-bipartite graphs $H$ (triangle-free graphs whose vertex set partitions into a stable set and a set inducing a graph of maximum degree at most one); in particular establishes it for the four-cycle and its complement.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; also referred to as the 'sparse linear conjecture' in the abstract
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Conjecture 1.4
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that in every $H$-free $\varepsilon$-bounded graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$ vertices, there is an anticomplete $(\varepsilon|G|, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair.
context_text: A graph $G$ is $\varepsilon$-bounded if its maximum degree is less than $\varepsilon|G|$. By a theorem of Rödl, $H$ satisfies Conjecture 1.3 if and only if both $H$ and $\bar{H}$ satisfy Conjecture 1.4, so the two conjectures are equivalent over all $H$. For certain graphs $H$, however, 1.4 is more tractable than 1.3.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; the paper proves this for all almost-bipartite $H$ as Theorem 1.5
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Open case H = K3 for Conjecture 1.4
statement_text: It remains open whether $H = K_3$ (the triangle) satisfies Conjecture 1.4.
context_text: The paper's main result establishes Conjecture 1.4 for all almost-bipartite graphs $H$; almost-bipartite graphs are triangle-free, so $K_3$ is the simplest graph not covered. This gap is noted both in the abstract and in the body of the paper.
notes: PDF source; stated informally as 'it remains open whether H = K_3 satisfies 1.4'
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Conjecture 3.3 (Conlon, Fox, Sudakov)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exist $\varepsilon, s > 0$ such that for every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$, and every $c$ with $0 \leq c \leq 1$, $G$ contains either a $c$-sparse or a $(1-c)$-dense $(\varepsilon c^s |G|,\, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair.
context_text: This strengthens the density theorem 3.2 of Erdős–Hajnal by requiring one of the two sets to have size linear in $|G|$, independent of $c$. It is attributed to Conlon, Sudakov, and Fox (problem 3.13 in [4]) and implies Conjecture 1.3. A pair $(A,B)$ is $c$-sparse if $|E(A,B)| \leq c|A||B|$ and $(1-c)$-dense if $|E(A,B)| \geq (1-c)|A||B|$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all almost-bipartite graphs $H$ as a consequence of the main theorem (Theorem 4.5).
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; same source reference [4] as Conjecture 1.3
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Conjecture 3.4
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exist $\varepsilon, s > 0$ such that for every $H$-free $\varepsilon$-bounded graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$, and every $c$ with $0 \leq c \leq 1$, there is a $c$-sparse $(\varepsilon c^s |G|,\, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair in $G$.
context_text: This is the $\varepsilon$-bounded specialization of Conjecture 3.3; for $\varepsilon$-bounded graphs the dense outcome is excluded. The implications $3.3 \Rightarrow 3.4 \Rightarrow 1.4$ hold. By analogy with 1.3 and 1.4, a theorem of Rödl implies that $H$ satisfies 3.3 if and only if both $H$ and $\bar{H}$ satisfy 3.4.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Conjecture 4.3
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exist $\varepsilon, s > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ with $|G| > 1$, and all $c$ with $0 \leq c \leq 1$, either $G$ is $(\varepsilon c^s, H)$-saturated, or there is a $c$-sparse or $(1-c)$-dense $(\varepsilon c^s |G|,\, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair in $G$.
context_text: A saturation strengthening of Conjecture 3.3: instead of requiring $G$ to be $H$-free, one only needs $G$ to not be $(\varepsilon c^s, H)$-saturated (i.e., to contain fewer than $\varepsilon c^s |G|^{|H|}$ induced copies of $H$). The implications $4.3 \Rightarrow 3.3$ and $4.3 \Rightarrow 4.4$ hold.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs

title: Conjecture 4.4
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exist $\varepsilon, s > 0$ such that for every $\varepsilon$-bounded graph $G$ and all $c$ with $0 \leq c \leq 1$, either $G$ is $(\varepsilon c^s, H)$-saturated, or there is a $c$-sparse $(\varepsilon c^s |G|,\, \varepsilon|G|)$-pair in $G$.
context_text: This is the saturation strengthening of Conjecture 3.4 and the $\varepsilon$-bounded specialisation of Conjecture 4.3. The paper proves this conjecture for all almost-bipartite graphs $H$ (Theorem 4.5), and shows that it becomes false if the $(\varepsilon c^s, H)$-saturation threshold is weakened to $\varepsilon$-saturation.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; proved for almost-bipartite H by Theorem 4.5 of this paper
paper_title: Sparse graphs with no polynomial-sized anticomplete pairs
